content: Brahmins are good followers, not leaders or thinkers when it comes to material reality. I heard this statement directly and indirectly from Brahmins who went abroad, and I found it quite convincing. In India, most Brahmins are very short-sighted, and it's because the caste system itself is built on short sightedness. The creators of such a system are also short-sighted. When the priestly class becomes the dominant class, society's priorities change from material conquest and material reality to overvaluing the scriptures, especially oral traditions, over evidence-based history and social reality.The epistemology is wrong.We need to change the epistemology to a Marxist one, at least if we want to see the facts. What many Brahmins value is not evidence-based history or philosophy; instead, they value oral traditions because that's what exists on the ground, and they call those oral traditions a religious philosophy. They convert everything into mythology and facts become optional.
comment: Broad identity-based claims often flatten real variance. In practice, epistemic failures usually track incentives and institutions more than group labels.
